title: Stage Application Group Confectionery - Zone Europe
description:
**Go and check the Nestlé Youth Entrepreneurship Platform:** **www.nestleyouthentrepreneurship.com**

**\#VisitNestlé \#NestléneedsYOUth \#Innovation \#YEP**

 **Position Snapshot**

* Nestlé in Italy, Assago (Milan) HQ
* Undergraduates or graduates in scientific subjects like Food Technology (preferred), Chemistry, Biology, Agronomy
* Nestlé welcomes people with disabilities
* Full\-time internship (6 months) \- We offer a monthly allowance of €900, free access to the company canteen, free access to a partner gym near the office and a company laptop
* **Start date: Mid\-September 2026**

 **Position Summary**


You will support the Application Group Confectionery Specialist for **Nestlé Professional** **Zone Europe** in product development activities, mainly focusing on document management across Nestlé and customer templates and portals

 **A day in the life of an Application Group Intern**

* Support product development and labelling activities, ensuring that all required technical documentation is provided by suppliers on time
* Support the drafting of Manufacturing Dossiers (MDs)
* Update Manufacturing Dossiers in case of minor changes (e.g., new multilingual labels)
* Store and/or update final Manufacturing Dossiers in the Nestlé Professional Zone Europe Co\-man SharePoint
* Update the Co\-man Master File, ensuring that documents are regularly reviewed and renewed upon expiry
* Support to guarantee the product compliance with Nestlé guidelines, uploading the semi\-finished goods specification and the recipes data in the Nestlé SAP database (Great/Formula one)
* Support the labelling process by sharing technical information with the Regulatory \& Scientific Affairs team
* Support compliance checks with Nestlé guidelines by uploading nutritional data into Nestlé databases for nutritional profiling and verification of nutrition declaration compliance
* Support drawing up and updating the technical sheets for the customers and sales teams
* Upload technical data to customers' portals
* Enter data into the Nestlé CAT2 tool for Central Technical Approval

 **What will make you successful?**

* Detail\-oriented, reliable and accurate approach to work
* Strong interpersonal and teamwork skills, with a proactive and flexible mindset
* Good organizational and problem\-solving abilities
* Good command of Microsoft Office Suite
* Fluent in both Italian\&English

 **Our Selection Process**


Step 1: Meeting with HR – Talent Acquisition motivational and attitudinal assessment


Step 2: Meeting with the Business (reference tutor) technical deep dive


Step 3: You will receive feedback, regardless of the outcome

 **Who We Are, What We Believe In**


We are Nestlé. We are the Good food, Good life company.


Good food, Good life is our promise and our daily commitment. At Nestlé you will Be a Force For Good by delivering on Nestlé purpose: unlocking the power of food and beverage to enhance quality of life – for individuals, families, communities and the planet – today and for generations to come.


At Nestlé, we promote an inclusive culture where you’re encouraged to be yourself and share your voice. We believe that recognition, inclusion and the valorisation of diversity help us grow — as individuals and as a company.


This is why you will find a leadership model that ensures equal opportunities for everyone at every organizational level.


Diversity is a fact. Inclusion is a choice — and we choose to be inclusive.

title: Compliance & Regulatory Affairs Manager
description:
We are a **European Cloud \& Cybersecurity Provider** supporting medium\-sized and large companies in managing and protecting their IT ecosystems.


Through highly reliable Cloud services, advanced Cybersecurity solutions, we help organizations ensure business continuity, security and digital innovation every day.


With offices across Italy, France, Spain and Switzerland, we combine an international mindset with strong local expertise, supporting customers through a team of skilled professionals and cutting\-edge technologies.


What drives us is not only what we do, but *how we do it*: we believe in **collaboration** across teams and countries, **transparency** in the way we work and communicate, and **accountability** in delivering concrete and reliable results for our internal and external customers.


As part of the growth of our **Corporate Legal Affairs** Department, we are looking for a Compliance \& Regulatory Affairs Manager responsible for overseeing the company's regulatory compliance in the technological and digital domain, leading complex compliance projects and balancing compliance needs with the business's transformation and growth objectives

  

Responsibilities of the selected candidate include:

* Coordinate projects to align company processes and policies with the main European digital regulatory frameworks: DORA, NIS 2 (Legislative Decree 138/2024 and ACN implementing rules), AI Act, Data Act, Cyber Resilience Act, AGID guidelines and implementing rules, GDPR.
* Assess the potential impact of new regulations and define adaptation plans of current company standards to avoid any delay in implementing all required measures \& carry out ex post analysis verifying the adequacy of processes and the effectiveness of controls
* Define and update company policies and procedures on ICT governance, cybersecurity, data governance and AI governance cooperating with all departments
* Develop and apply compliance risk assessment methodologies, including AI risk assessment and third\-party risk management.
* Manage relationships with supervisory and sector regulatory authorities, coordinating responses, institutional communications and compliance obligations.
* Support business and technology functions in embedding compliance requirements into processes and services (compliance by design).
* Define, together with HRO, contents and timings for training on RA compliance within Reevo Group.Participate, together with other Sales and Channel sales teams, to the definition of the Sales strategy in order to maximize results.

  

The ideal candidate meets the following requirements:

**Hard Skills:**

* At least 5\-7 years in RA compliance and/or risk management roles, preferably in regulated sectors (financial, insurance, telecoms, cloud/cybersecurity) or specialist consulting firms.
* Experience leading end\-to\-end regulatory compliance projects, with project management skills and coordination of cross\-functional stakeholders.
* Experience reporting to corporate bodies and managing relationships with supervisory authorities.
* In\-depth knowledge of the main European digital regulations: GDPR, DORA, NIS 2, AI Act, Data Act, Cyber Resilience Act and of AGID guidelines and implementing rules.


For each framework the following depth of knowledge is needed:


(i) NIS 2 / D.Lgs. 138/2024: full command of the ACN implementing rules (*Determinazioni ACN*), categorization workflow, minimum security measures across relevance levels, supply chain obligations, and incident notification timelines;


(ii) AGID: knowledge of AGID Guidelines on PEC, interoperability, cloud qualification, and security measures applicable to public\-administration suppliers;


(iii) AI Act: risk\-classification logic, obligations for high\-risk AI systems, conformity assessment procedures, and requirements for general\-purpose AI models;


(iv) Data Act and Data Governance Act: data\-sharing obligations, access rights of users and public bodies, contractual requirements, and interplay with GDPR;


(v) GDPR: accountability framework, DPIA methodology, records of processing activities, data breach management, and DPA interaction;


(vi) DORA: ICT risk management framework, ICT third\-party risk, TLPT requirements, and register of contracts with ICT providers.
* Familiarity with cybersecurity governance, ICT risk and data governance frameworks. Preferred: knowledge of GRC systems and compliance monitoring tools.

**Language Skills:**

* Good command of Italian and English (C1\), both written and spoken is mandatory;
* Good command of French and/or Spanish is a plus.

**Soft Skills:**

* Leadership and management of complex projects;
* cross\-functional stakeholder coordination;
* strong analytical and problem\-solving skills;
* communication and relationship management

 **Why Join Us:**


We believe people perform at their best when they can grow, collaborate and maintain a healthy work\-life balance.  

For this reason, we offer:

* Flexible working approach and smart working opportunities;
* Meal vouchers;
* Welfare programs;
* Health and accidentes insurance coverage;
* Dedicate budget for learning, growth and development;
* Compensation and benefit packages aligned with role, responsibilities and local policies.

***If you enjoy challenges and actively contribute to company success, we are the right place for you!***

 **Workplace**: Vimercate (MB)/Hybrid

 **How to Apply**


Please send your CV and a short presentation to: https://careers.reevo.eu/job\-seekers.php

  

\#LI\-NB1

  


  

Settore: Informatica


Ruolo: Altro


CCNL applicato: COMMERCIO \- Confcommercio


Salario mensile: EUR 3600 \- EUR 4400


Tipo di occupazione: Contratto a tempo indeterminato

**At Recordati, we are united by a clear purpose:** unlocking the full potential of life. With a century of history behind us, we are building the next chapter of our journey, continuing to grow while keeping patients at the centre of all we do.
  
  

What drives us is a shared belief that health, and the opportunity to live life to the full, should be within reach for everyone. This brings energy to our work, shaping how we support each other and show up every day. Our values – Better Together, Never Settle and Always Deliver – reflect this mindset, helping us to keep improving and make a meaningful difference.
  
  

Above all, these values bring to life our promise to everyone at Recordati, you can unlock your full potential. We know that people thrive when they can be themselves, are trusted and supported, and are given the opportunity to grow and make a meaningful contribution.
  
  

Are you ready to unlock your full potential?
  
  

Job Purpose
  
  

Lo Specialista Assicurazione Qualità garantisce che i processi produttivi, analitici e documentali siano conformi alle normative vigenti (GMP, GDP, normative AIFA) e agli standard di qualità aziendali.
  
  

.\-.\-.\-.\-.\-
  
  

The Quality Assurance Specialist ensures that manufacturing, analytical and documentation processes comply with applicable regulations (GMP, GDP, AIFA) and internal quality standards.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
Garantire la conformità dei processi alle normative GMP e alle procedure interne
  
Gestione Change Control
  
Collaborare con Produzione, Controllo Qualità e Regulatory Affairs
  
Revisionare anagrafiche e ricette (incluso il flusso degli Art Work)
  
Revisionare Audit Trail di sistema e gestire aspetti di Data Integrity
  
Revisionare Master Batch Record
  
Supporto nella Scrittura di protocolli/report di convalida
  
Effettuare indagini (deviazioni e/o reclami) e redigere i relativi report
  
  

.\-.\-.\-.\-.\-
  
  

Ensure compliance of processes with GMP regulations and internal procedures
  
Change Control Management
  
Collaborate with Manufacturing, Quality Control and Regulatory Affairs
  
Review of Master Data and Recipe (including Art Work flow)
  
Review Audit Trail and Data Integrity management
  
Review and manage the flows of the Master Batch Records
  
Support in Drafting of validation protocol/report
  
Investigation and related reporting (complaints and/or deviation)
  
Required Education
  
Laurea in discipline scientifiche (Chimica, CTF, Farmacia, Biologia o affini.
  
  

.\-.\-.\-.\-.\-
  
  

Degree in a scientific discipline (Chemistry, Pharmaceutical Sciences, Biology or equivalent)
  
Required Skills and Experience
  
Esperienza di almeno 2\-3 anni in Assicurazione Qualità in ambito chimico\-farmaceutico
  
Conoscenza delle GMP e delle normative di settore
  
  

.\-.\-.\-.\-.\-
  
  

At least 2\-3 years of experience in Quality Assurance within the pharmaceutical or chemical industry
  
Knowledge of GMP and applicable regulations
  
Required Languages
  
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese
  
  

.\-.\-.\-.\-.\-.
  
  

Good command of the English language
  
Trattamento economico
  
  

**RAL (Retribuzione Annua Lorda) di partenza:** € 35\.000
  
  

**Nota bene:** Il trattamento economico indicato si applica esclusivamente alle posizioni regolate dal contratto collettivo di lavoro, in conformità con le vigenti normative sulla trasparenza retributiva. L'offerta finale sarà definita in base all'esperienza, alle competenze professionali e all'esito del percorso di selezione, salvaguardando l'equità interna. In Recordati valorizziamo le persone attraverso un sistema di remunerazione complessivo, equo e competitivo, che verrà presentato nel dettaglio durante le fasi di colloquio.
  
  

.\-.\-.\-.\-
  
  

**Gross Annual Salary starting from:** € 35,000
  
  

**Please note:** The salary indicated in this posting complies with local pay transparency regulations for Italian employment contracts. Final offers will be tailored to the successful candidate's experience, professional expertise, and interview journey, while ensuring internal equity. At Recordati, we are committed to a comprehensive, fair, and competitive remuneration system; a full overview of your total rewards package will be shared and discussed with you during the recruiting process.

title: PM/PMO Consultant area Banking (Luxembourg Projects)
Regulatory Affairs Intern
description:
**FRAMESI S.p.A.**, storica realtà italiana nel settore cosmetico professionale, da oltre 80 anni sviluppa e produce **prodotti innovativi per la cura e la bellezza dei capelli**, distribuiti a livello internazionale. Per il nostro stabilimento produttivo di Paderno Dugnano, siamo alla ricerca di un/a **TIROCINANTE REGULATORY AFFAIRS.** 

La risorsa affiancherà il team Affari Regolatori nelle attività di gestione della conformità normativa dei prodotti cosmetici destinati ai mercati internazionali.

**Principali attività**

* Monitoraggio della normativa cosmetica dei Paesi extra UE.
* Supporto nella predisposizione della documentazione per la registrazione dei prodotti nei mercati esteri.
* Redazione e aggiornamento di dossier tecnici, Product Information File (PIF) e documentazione regolatoria.
* Preparazione di certificati e documentazione tecnica a supporto delle esportazioni.
* Valutazione della documentazione tossicologica di materie prime e prodotti finiti.
* Verifica degli impianti stampa e delle informazioni riportate in etichetta.
* Gestione delle richieste provenienti dai distributori esteri e collaborazione con il reparto Export.

**Requisiti**

* Laurea in CTF, Farmacia, Chimica, Biologia, Biotecnologie o discipline scientifiche affini.
* Master in Regulatory Affairs, Scienze e Tecnologie Cosmetiche o percorso di specializzazione equivalente.
* Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese e spagnola
* Buona conoscenza del pacchetto Office.
* Precisione, organizzazione e attitudine al lavoro in team.

**Cosa offriamo**

* Percorso di formazione in un contesto internazionale.
* Affiancamento da parte di professionisti del settore.
* Opportunità di sviluppare competenze specialistiche nell'ambito Regulatory Affairs applicato al settore cosmetico.

Se desideri intraprendere il tuo percorso professionale nel mondo degli Affari Regolatori nel mondo della cosmetica, inviaci la tua candidatura.

\- I*l presente annuncio si rivolge ai candidati di ambo i sessi (L. 903/77\).*

title: Junior Management Consulting (Luxembourg Projects)
description:
**Chi siamo:**  

Oggi CRPI Digital è la capofila di un gruppo interamente dedicato all’innovazione tecnologica, alla transizione digitale e al business consulting. Rappresenta l’evoluzione naturale di un percorso imprenditoriale avviato nel 2002 con la fondazione di Interlem, prima società del gruppo tutt’ora operativa, da cui eredita una solida tradizione di competenze nell’ambito dell’ICT, della trasformazione digitale e della consulenza strategica.
  
  

Il Gruppo continua a crescere e a innovare con l’obiettivo di rispondere in modo sempre più efficace e sostenibile alle sfide del mercato e della società. Crediamo che il valore di un’impresa non si misuri solo nei risultati economici, ma anche nella capacità di generare un impatto positivo sui territori e sulle comunità in cui opera.
  
  

Da novembre 2024, CRPI Digital fa parte del network Lounge Elite di Intesa Sanpaolo presso Borsa Italiana – Gruppo Euronext.
  
  

**Opportunità lavorativa:**  

Avvant è la struttura in forte crescita dedicata ai servizi di Advisory \& Consulting di CRPI Digital. Attraverso un approccio di management consulting strategico, Avvant accompagna le aziende nel percorso della Twin Transition, promuovendo l’integrazione tra trasformazione digitale e sostenibilità.
  
  

**Le principali aree di intervento di Avvant includono:** Executive Advice, AI \& Data Intelligence, Cybersecurity Advisory, Innovation \& Sustainability, Cloud Strategy.
  
  

Siamo alla ricerca di persone che assumano il ruolo di Junior Management Consultant.
  
  

**Principali Responsabilità:**  

Interagire e collaborare con le figure Senior del team per interagire con i C\-level e/o con gli stakeholder decisionali, mantenendo relazioni di fiducia e offrendo consulenza strategica.
  
Svolgere attività di governance in particolare garantendo lo sviluppo e l'esecuzione di strategie, piani e attività di trasformazione e gestione del cambiamento in linea con gli obiettivi del Cliente ed i requisiti progettuali che declinano tali obiettivi.
  
Disegnare processi To\-Be e Target Operating Model, identificando opportunità di miglioramento e di ottimizzazione e digitalizzazion da un punto di vista di processi, organizzazione, sistemi.
  
Interagire con tutte le Società del Gruppo CRPI valorizzando tutte le sinergie che possano scaricare a terra il valore delle soluzioni proposte ed offerte.
  
Gestire la Governance di programmi e/o di progetti quale punti di orchestrazione dei vari referenti e fornitori che afferiscono agli stessi.
  
Guidare e supervisionare team di progetto, garantendo l'eccellenza nella delivery.
  
Assistere nel monitoraggio e nella misurazione dell'efficacia degli sforzi di gestione del cambiamento, raccogliendo dati e approfondimenti per supportare il miglioramento continuo del Cliente declinandolo concretamente e proattivamente in obiettivi e piani di attività.
  
  

**Cosa offriamo:**  

Retribuzione competitiva ai massimi livelli, commisurata all’esperienza e all’alto profilo del ruolo;
  
Ambiente dinamico, orientato all’innovazione continua e alla valorizzazione del talento;
  
Percorsi strutturati di formazione e aggiornamento, con supporto concreto al conseguimento delle principali certificazioni professionali;
  
Partecipazione attiva a progetti ad alto contenuto di innovazione e rilevanza strategica, a contatto con clienti di primario livello.
  
  

**Aree progettuali :**  

Crediti (Lending \& Credit Management);
  
Financial Markets \& Treasury;
  
Regulatory \& Compliance (incl. DORA, AML, KYC);
  
Risk Management (Credit, Market, Operational Risk);
  
Legal \& Corporate Affairs;
  
Payments \& Transaction Banking;
  
Supply Chain Finance;
  
Trade \& Export Finance;
  
Wealth Management \& Private Banking;
  
Governance, Organization \& Transformation;
  
Finance \& Planning (Controlling, Reporting, ALM);
  
Data, Reporting \& Regulatory Reporting.
  
  

**Requisiti:**  

Laurea Magistrale in ambito STEM o Economia. Il conseguimento di un master e/o un MBA sarà considerato un plus.
  
Verrà considerato un plus il possedere certificazioni specifiche (es: PMP Certification, Scrum Certification).
  
Almeno 1 \- 2 anni di esperienza nel ruolo preferibilmente con esposizione internazionale.
  
Comprovata esperienza nel guidare con successo iniziative di gestione del cambiamento dall'ideazione al completamento nell'ambito della trasformazione digitale.
  
L’aver maturato esperienza in progetti SAP, Data Intelligence, Business Intelligence verrà considerato un elemento distintivo.
  
Uso fluente della lingua inglese sia scritta che parlata nei contesti lavorativi. La conoscenza di altre lingue verrà considerata un plus.
  
Aver maturato esperienze lavorative in contesti internazionali/esteri
  
Ottime capacità relazionali, comunicative, di coordinamento e gestione delle attività e dei team.
  
  

**Retribuzione:**  

**RAL:** 23000\- 28000 in base all' esperienza acquisita.
  
  

**Sede di Lavoro:**  

**Sede di lavoro:** Lussemburgo
  
  

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
  
  

Il presente annuncio è rivolto a candidati ambosessi in conformità al D.Lgs. n.198 del 2006 e ai D.Lgs. n.215 e n.216 in tema di parità di trattamento.
  
I dati richiesti verranno trattati nell’assoluto rispetto delle disposizioni contenute nel Regolamento Europeo 679/2016 (General Data Protection Regulation \- “GDPR” o “Normativa Privacy”) e sue successive modificazioni ed integrazioni.

title: Medical Affairs Internship
description:
**Medical Affairs Intern**  

**Milan, Italy**

**About the job**


Looking to launch your career at the cutting edge of healthcare? Join Sanofi for a chance to develop with mentoring and guidance from inspirational leaders while helping to make an impact on the lives of countless people worldwide. As **Medical Affairs Intern**, you’ll be part of a **highly specialized team** and you’ll learn to manage **scientific activities** related to ongoing projects in Medical Affairs with a special focus on **Respiratory indications.**


We are an innovative global healthcare company with one purpose: ***to chase the miracles of science to improve people’s lives*****.** We’re also a company where you can flourish and grow your career, with countless opportunities to explore, make connections with people, and stretch the limits of what you thought was possible. *Ready to get started?*

**You will be involved in the following activities**

* Support the **Respiratory Medical Affairs team** by collaborating closely with the Medical Team and other departments such as Marketing, Market Access, Medical Information and Regulatory Affairs.
* Collaborate in organizing and **preparing materials** for **medical meetings, webinars, advisory boards internal and external meeting**s.
* Assist with scientific activities, including **medical consultancies**, **reviewing promotional materials, updating clinical trial status and conducting scientific literature research.**
* Participate in conference calls and collaborate on cross\-functional activities and projects.

**About you**

* Master’s degree in **Medicine**, **Pharmacy, Biotecnology** or related **fields**
* Fluent **English** (level B2\+/C1 or higher)
* Good knowledge of **Excel** and **PowerPoint**
* **Accuracy** and **organizational** skills, passion for **scientific literature** and teamwork

**Why choose us?**

* Bring the **miracles of science** to life alongside a supportive, future\-focused team.
* Start your career with **inspiring mentors** who will help you take your first professional steps.
* You will have the possibility to carry out the internship in a **hybrid mode**.
* We offer an **internship lasting 6 months**. Competitive monthly compensation and meal vouchers will be provided.


\#LI\-Hybrid


\#EarlyTalent

**Pursue** ***Progress*****. Discover** ***Extraordinary*****.**


Progress doesn’t happen without people – people from different backgrounds, in different locations, doing different roles, all united by one thing: a desire to make miracles happen. You can be one of those people. Chasing change, embracing new ideas and exploring all the opportunities we have to offer. Let’s pursue Progress. And let’s discover Extraordinary together.


At Sanofi, we provide equal opportunities to all regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, or gender identity.  

  

Watch our ALL IN video and check out our Diversity Equity and Inclusion actions at sanofi.com!

title: Medical Science Liaison Rare Neurology (Lombardia, Veneto, Trentin- Alto Adige, Friuli-Venezia Giulia, Emilia-Romagna)
description:
**Location** Milan, Lombardy, Italy **Job ID** R\-254857 **Date posted** 29/06/2026
  
Are you ready to elevate scientific engagement in rare neurology and help shape standards of care for patients across Northern Italy? Do you thrive at the intersection of rigorous science, cross‑functional collaboration, and field leadership that turns insights into better outcomes?


As our **Medical Science Liaison in Rare Neurology,** you will be the trusted scientific partner across Lombardia, Veneto, Trentino‑Alto Adige, Friuli‑Venezia Giulia, and Emilia Romagna. You will build and deepen relationships with leading clinicians and researchers, accelerate the flow of high‑quality medical information, and translate real‑world insights into actions that advance patient care. Your work will directly influence research, evidence generation, and scientific exchange that matters in clinics and communities. You will join a rare disease medical team with the energy of a biotech and the reach of a global organization.


In this role, you will set a high bar for excellence in execution, provide mentorship across the field team, and contribute to strategies and programs that improve outcomes for underserved patient populations.


**Accountabilities:**


**KOL Engagement and Scientific Exchange:** Build trusted, long‑term relationships with regional KOLs; deliver timely, balanced, and compliant medical information; and drive high‑quality medical‑to‑medical interactions that deepen disease and treatment understanding.


**Evidence Generation and Research Support**: Plan and support research initiatives for assigned products; partner with Italian centers involved in national and international clinical development; and collaborate closely with Clinical Operations to ensure quality, compliance, and impact.


**Local Study Excellence:** Support the Medical Advisor on local studies by shaping protocols, enabling accurate data management and analysis, preparing final reports, and ensuring timely, ethical dissemination of results.


**Insight to Strategy:** Systematically capture medical insights from field interactions and translate them into clear, data‑driven recommendations that inform strategy and elevate scientific impact in partnership with the Medical Advisor.


**Regulatory and Commercial Partnership:** Provide high‑quality support to Regulatory Affairs on registration dossiers and related documents; partner with Marketing to uphold scientific and educational standards through material review and the planning of scientific events.


**Congress and Medical Programs Leadership:** Lead the medical presence at national and European congresses; staff medical booths; facilitate advisory boards; and guide engagement with local scientific societies to strengthen peer‑to‑peer exchange.


**Data, Digital, and AI Enablement:** Define outcome metrics and KPIs; present data‑driven results and recommendations to leadership; design or recommend digital implementations (e.g., Veeva, Salesforce, Tableau) and AI use cases for Medical Affairs; contribute to SOP updates and best‑practice playbooks.


**Regional Ownership and Compliance:** Manage all activities and account relationships in line with Company Values, Code of Conduct, internal procedures, and Italian legislation; ensure meticulous governance in every interaction across the region.


**Leadership and Mentorship:** Mentor new hires and less‑experienced MSLs through onboarding, coaching, and ongoing development; oversee specific processes and projects across the MSL team to support the Therapeutic Area Lead and drive excellence in execution.


**Autonomy and Accountability:** Operate independently with limited guidance; take full accountability for results; and consistently raise the bar on operational performance and scientific impact across Northern Italy.


**Essential Skills/Experience:**


* Proven Medical Affairs/MSL experience: 2\+ year in Medical Affairs/MSL roles.
* Strategic insight generation: demonstrated ability to synthesize insights from KOL engagements, advisory boards, congresses, and medical inquiries into actionable, compliant recommendations that improve patient care.
* Strong leadership and interpersonal skills, including feedback delivery, rolemodeling best practices, and fostering a collaborative learning culture.
* Independent working style and accountability: ability to operate with limited oversight, set and track outcome metrics/KPIs, and present datadriven results and recommendations to TAL/leadership.
* Digital and data proficiency: familiarity with Veeva, Salesforce; capability to lead AI/datadriven pilots and contribute to change management.
* Crossfunctional collaboration: effective partnership with Medical Advisors and broader teams (e.g., Clinical, Market Access, Regulatory, Compliance, Marketing).
* Compliance and ethical standards: strong knowledge of applicable regulations and legislation relevant to pharmaceutical industry.
* Scientific communication: excellent written and verbal communication in English and Italian; ability to facilitate complex medical discussions and deliver clear presentations to diverse audiences.
* Project and research support skills: experience in protocol planning, data management and analysis, reporting, and timely publication processes.
* Advanced scientific degree (MD, PharmD, PhD, or equivalent) in Neurology, Neuroscience, or related biomedical discipline.

**Desirable Skills/Experience:**


* Field experience in rare neurology; direct exposure in clinical research, evidence generation, or medical education.
* Established network with neurologists and multidisciplinary centers across Lombardia, Veneto, Trentino‑Alto Adige, Friuli‑Venezia Giulia, and Emilia Romagna.
* Track record leading congress activities, advisory boards, and medical programs at national or European level.
* Experience with real‑world evidence generation, registries, and outcomes research in rare disease.
* Advanced analytics capability with CRM/analytics tools and dashboards (e.g., Veeva reporting, Salesforce analytics, Tableau) and familiarity with medical‑compliant AI applications.
* Strong project leadership in cross‑functional settings including Regulatory, Market Access, and Compliance during pre‑ and post‑launch phases.
* Additional European language proficiency beyond Italian and English.

**Why AstraZeneca:** Join a rare disease team where bold science and swift execution meet real patient stories. You will work in an environment that blends the autonomy and pace of a biotech with the resources and reach of a global organization, bringing unexpected teams together in the same room to unlock new ideas and accelerate impact. We pair ambition with care—valuing kindness alongside high standards—so you can stretch your capabilities with tailored development while staying close to the lived experiences that guide our work. Here, your expertise turns into action that shapes evidence, informs decisions, and brings therapies to people who need them most.


**Call to Action**: Ready to lead scientific engagement in rare neurology and help define standards of care across Northern Italy? Step forward and shape what comes next with us.

title: Senior Medical Advisor Rare Hematology, Nephrology, Transplant and Amyloidosis
description:
**Location** Milan, Lombardy, Italy **Job ID** R\-253785 **Date posted** 29/06/2026
  
Are you ready to turn complex evidence into clear action that improves outcomes for people living with rare conditions? Do you want to shape the scientific direction of **Rare Hematology, Nephrology, Transplant and Amyloidosis** by partnering closely with leading clinicians and cross\-functional teams? Based in Milan and reporting to the Medical Affairs Lead for this therapy area, you will be instrumental in connecting cutting\-edge science to real\-world practice.


This role anchors the medical plan and steers impactful launches, evidence generation, and education. You will drive scientifically robust and compliant engagements, elevate external credibility, and deliver insights that accelerate timely diagnosis and effective treatment. With access to global expertise and fast\-moving teams, you will help translate biology into meaningful change where the need is greatest.


Operating across medical affairs, clinical research, market access and commercial collaboration, you will lead projects end\-to\-end—from strategy through execution—while strengthening our presence in the rare disease community. How will you build trusted partnerships that raise the standard of care and bring patients closer to the therapies they need?


**Accountabilities:**


\-Launch Excellence: Lead and support pre\- and post\-approval activities by mapping the scientific landscape, defining unmet needs, and ensuring launch readiness and execution that is rigorous, compliant, and aligned to medical strategy.


* KOL Strategy and Engagement: Identify, map, and cultivate relationships with Key Opinion Leaders to generate insights, inform strategy, and enhance scientific credibility.


* Medical Plan and Project Leadership: Own the medical plan and serve as project lead for local clinical studies, observational research, and diagnostic screening initiatives, delivering on time and with measurable impact.


* Scientific Leadership and Expertise: Maintain deep, up\-to\-date knowledge of products and disease biology in Rare Hematology, Nephrology, Transplant and Amyloidosis; translate literature into clear guidance, priorities, and actions.


* Compliance and Governance: Uphold local laws, guidelines, codes of practice, and internal policies; ensure promotional and educational activities meet ethical and regulatory standards.


* Medical Education and Materials: Develop scientific content for internal and external education; provide expert input to the development, review, and approval of materials for scientific meetings and symposia.


* Scientific Exchange and Insights: Deliver reactive, on\-site medical presentations; participate in local meetings to educate, gather intelligence, and channel insights to shape strategy.


* Pharmacovigilance Collaboration: Partner with safety teams to address medical issues related to adverse events and strengthen risk communication and patient safety.


* Commercial Partnership: Collaborate with marketing and sales to create scientifically sound messages, documentation, and tailored projects that remain aligned with medical strategy.


* Market Access Support: Contribute medical and scientific evidence to value propositions; support cross\-functional discussions and activities that demonstrate clinical value and facilitate access.


* Training and Capability Building: Support the Therapeutic Area Lead in medical training for the sales force; review promotional materials in accordance with local regulatory requirements.


* Professional Engagement: Participate in professional organizations and external forums to enhance field insights and advance professional development.


**Essential Skills/Experience:**


* Experience in product launches (both pre\-launch preparation and post\-launch excellence) in different therapeutic areas


* Business\-ready, hands\-on, and high execution capability with the ability to navigate complex environments effectively.


* Proven ability to identify, map, and develop Key Opinion Leaders and maintain strong scientific relationships.


* Flexible and agile, with experience acting as project manager for medical plans, local clinical studies, observational studies, and diagnostic screening projects with cross\-functional collaboration.


* Demonstrated capability to develop and maintain scientific expertise and familiarity with literature regarding products and the relevant therapeutic disease area.


* Strong knowledge of local laws and guidelines, codes of practice, and company policies related to promotional materials, activities, and educational programs.


* Experience developing educational materials for internal and external medical initiatives in relevant therapeutic areas.


* Track record of delivering reactive medical presentations to customers on site.


* Experience supporting pharmacovigilance activities and managing medical issues related to adverse events.


* Experience collaborating with country marketing and sales teams on promotional messaging, supportive documentation, and customized projects.


* Experience providing scientific support for the development, review, and approval of materials for scientific meetings, symposia, and other medical education activities.


* Experience participating in local meetings for educational purposes and to gather medical intelligence and insights.


* Experience supporting a Therapeutic Area Lead in delivering medical training to sales teams.


* Experience supporting promotional review processes in alignment with local regulatory requirements.


**Desirable Skills/Experience:**


* Strong solid scientific background (Advanced Scientific or Medical Degree/PhD)


* Senior Medical Affairs professional, with at least 5 years' experience in a biotech or pharmaceutical industry experience


* Strong expertise in amyloidosis, cardiology, and hematology


* Familiarity with diagnostic pathways, screening programs, and real\-world evidence methodologies


* Sound knowledge of Good Clinical Practice and protocol development


* Excellent scientific communication skills in both local language and English


* Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, matrixed environment and translate complex data into clear, actionable insights


* Ability to use AI tools to rapidly synthesize scientific evidence and interpret medical insights; leverage analytics to identify HCP trends, scenario analysis, and development of medical communication, combined with strong critical judgement to validate AI\-generated output and ensure scientific credibility, patient focus, and compliance


**Why AstraZeneca:**


Join a rare disease team that pairs the agility of a biotech with the scale and capabilities of a global biopharmaceutical leader. We stay close to patients and their care teams, letting lived experience shape our decisions and sharpen our focus on what matters most. You will work with unexpected combinations of experts in the same room to unleash bold thinking, supported by leaders who value kindness alongside ambition. Expect hands\-on collaboration with top scientific voices, opportunities to drive evidence from concept to practice, and a working rhythm that blends on\-site collaboration in Milan several days each week with flexibility—so you can move quickly, learn fast, and see your impact ripple from strategy to bedside.


**Call to Action:**


Step into a role where your science changes lives—bring your expertise to AstraZeneca and help accelerate better care for people with rare diseases today!

Full\-time


Permanent position

In addition to the Gross Base Salary, we offer:


* An annual target bonus, governed by the Company’s policies as amended from time to time and subject to periodic review
* Wellbeing benefits (welfare, insurance, psychological support, etc.)
* Company Car

Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to skills, experience, internal organizational equity and other relevant factor.

title: Regulatory Affairs Specialist (medical device / cosmetici)
description:
**Olcelli Farmaceutici** è una storica e consolidata realtà italiana specializzata nello sviluppo e nella produzione di specialità farmaceutiche, dispositivi medici e cosmetici. Coniughiamo tradizione industriale e innovazione costante, ponendo la massima attenzione alla qualità e alla sicurezza. Per il potenziamento del nostro team, cerchiamo un/a Regulatory Affairs Specialist.

Inserito/a nel Team Quality \& Regulatory, la risorsa gestirà la conformità normativa dei prodotti aziendali. In particolare si occuperà di:

* **Dispositivi Medici:** Gestione strategica e operativa della **transizione normativa** (adeguamento ai requisiti del Regolamento MDR 2017/745\), redazione/aggiornamento dei Technical File e della documentazione di sorveglianza post\-market (PMS/PMCF). Gestione delle registrazioni su EUDAMED e Ministero della Salute.
* **Cosmetici:** Validazione delle formule, revisione del packaging/etichettatura (claim) e redazione/mantenimento del PIF (Product Information File). Gestione delle notifiche sul portale CPNP.
* **Interfaccia Enti Esterni:** Relazione con Organismi Notificati, laboratori e autorità regolatorie
* **Audit e Qualifica Fornitori:** Pianificazione e conduzione di **audit ispettivi presso i fornitori** (di materie prime, packaging e servizi critici) per garantirne la qualifica e il mantenimento degli standard qualitativi richiesti.
* **Conformità Chimica (REACH e CLP):** Verifica della conformità delle materie prime e delle sostanze chimiche in conformità ai regolamenti REACH e CLP. Supervisione e aggiornamento delle Schede di Dati di Sicurezza (SDS) e corretta classificazione/etichettatura dei prodotti.

Retribuzione: €30\.000,00 \- €60\.000,00 all'anno

title: Cluster V&A Director
description:
**Cluster V\&A Director**
at Kyowa Kirin International plc
Milan \- Italy **WE PUSH THE BOUNDARIES OF MEDICINE.**  

**LEAPING FORWARD TO MAKE PEOPLE SMILE**  

At Kyowa Kirin International (KKI), our purpose is to make people smile. This means more than drug discovery and development; it is about embedding care into everything we do to make a difference every day for those that need it most. We’re an inclusive pharmaceutical company that takes time to understand what really matters to our patients, their families, and their healthcare professionals, helping our people to take bold actions that deliver life\-changing solutions sooner. Our culture is rooted in our values: Teamwork, Commitment to Life, Innovation, and Integrity. They help us to push boundaries to deliver extraordinary impact and make KKI a brilliant place to work. **Job Purpose**  

As Cluster Value \& Access Director – Southern Cluster, you will be at the forefront of improving patient access to Kyowa Kirin’s innovative medicines. In this highly visible leadership role, you will develop and execute market access, pricing, and payer engagement strategies that support both current and future products.
Partnering with local, regional, and global teams, you will translate insights into impactful value propositions, lead HTA and reimbursement activities, and shape access strategies that deliver sustainable value for patients, healthcare systems, and Kyowa Kirin. This is an exciting opportunity to influence key access decisions and drive commercial success across the Southern Cluster. **Key Responsibilities:*** Shape and deliver pricing, reimbursement, and market access strategies across the Southern Cluster (Italy, Spain, Portugal), driving optimal patient access and commercial success.
* Develop compelling value propositions, HTA submissions, value dossiers, and HEOR materials to support reimbursement and pricing objectives.
* Partner with affiliate, regional, and global teams to anticipate market access challenges and translate insights into actionable strategies.
* Lead payer engagement initiatives and innovative access solutions that maximize product value and reimbursement opportunities.
* Monitor healthcare policies, market dynamics, and pricing trends to identify opportunities and risks across the portfolio.
* Collaborate cross\-functionally with Regulatory, Medical, Clinical, and Commercial teams to ensure evidence generation meets payer needs.
* Serve as the subject matter expert for Value \& Access and Health Economics \& Outcomes Research (HEOR), providing strategic guidance across the organization.
* Support pipeline planning by assessing future market access requirements and influencing global development strategies.
* Lead and contribute to strategic market access projects while promoting Kyowa Kirin’s values and reputation internally and externally.

 **Position Requirements**  

* Advanced degree in Health Sciences, Economics, Pharmacy, Medicine, or a related field (PhD and/or postgraduate qualifications in Market Access, HEOR, or Business preferred).
* Significant experience in pharmaceutical Market Access, Pricing \& Reimbursement, Government Affairs, or HEOR, with a strong track record of successful access outcomes.
* Deep understanding of reimbursement systems, health economics, pricing strategies, and payer decision\-making.
* Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive cross\-functional collaboration in complex environments.
* Strong commercial acumen, strategic thinking, and project leadership skills.
* Experience developing HEOR evidence and supporting market access submissions.
* Fluent in English and the local cluster language; additional languages are an advantage.
* Existing network within healthcare authorities and payer organizations is highly desirable.

### **Key Competencies**


✔ Strategic and commercially minded  

✔ Strong leadership, communication, and negotiation skills  

✔ Highly analytical with excellent problem\-solving abilities  

✔ Influential relationship builder across internal and external stakeholders  

✔ Self\-motivated, proactive, and results\-driven  

✔ Innovative thinker with a patient\-focused mindset  

✔ Comfortable working independently and in cross\-functional teams  

✔ Strong project and budget management capabilities  

✔ Embodies Kyowa Kirin's values of **Commitment to Life, Teamwork, Innovation, and Integrity**.

Senior Consultant/Assistant Manager Governance, Risk & Compliance - Financial Services
description:
**Senior Consultant/Assistant Manager \- Governance, Risk \& Compliance \- Financial Services**

  


*The chance to build a better future is right in front of you.*

  


***Do Work That Matters*** **\- Il tuo ruolo e le tue responsabilità**

  



Vuoi fare un lavoro che sia davvero significativo e di impatto? In KPMG avrai l’opportunità di aiutare i clienti, la società, le colleghe e i colleghi ad affrontare e risolvere le sfide più attuali e complesse. **KPMG Advisory** offre servizi di consulenza alle imprese per affiancarle nella trasformazione del business model, nel miglioramento e nella digitalizzazione dei processi, nell'analisi e nella gestione dei rischi, nonché supporto nelle operazioni di finanza straordinaria.

  



In particolare, entrando a far parte del Team di **Governance, Risk \& Compliance** di **Milano** potrai aiutare le aziende a trasformare i **rischi in opportunità** per migliorare la loro organizzazione e l’evoluzione dei loro sistemi di governance, ottenendo un vantaggio competitivo e offrendo soluzioni integrate e approcci innovativi per la gestione strategica del rischio.

  



Nello specifico, ti occuperai di:


* **Internal governance** e sistema dei **controlli interni** (es. **Solvency 2**/ **CRD IV**)
* Customer protection (es. **IDD/** **Mifid 2**)
* Sostenibilità (es. Reg UE 2019/2088 e 2020/852\)
* **Responsabilità amministrativa degli enti ex D. Lgs 231/01**
* Sistema dei **controlli interni degli intermediari bancari, finanziari ed assicurativi**

  



Sarai coinvolta/o su **progetti internazionali** **e nazionali** al fianco di professioniste/i che ti aiuteranno ad aumentare le tue conoscenze professionali e relazionali.

  


***Come As You Are \- Le tue competenze e caratteristiche***

  



Questa opportunità è perfetta per te se hai:

  


* Esperienza di **almeno 2 anni** presso aziende (compagnie assicurative o banche) o presso società di consulenza nell’area Risk \& Compliance;
* **Laurea magistrale o Master in Economia** o **Giurisprudenza** con ottima votazione;
* Interesse per i **Financial Services** (settore bancario e/o assicurativo, asset \& wealth management) e buona conoscenza del framework normativo del settore finanziario;
* Ottima conoscenza della **lingua inglese**, meglio se avvalorata da un’esperienza di studio e/o lavoro all’estero;

  


***Your Future With Us \- Cosa troverai in KPMG***

  


* Un ambiente di **lavoro inclusivo**, equo e che rispetta, incoraggia e valorizza le **diversità**
* Un contesto stimolante e dinamico che incentiva la **creatività** e il **lavoro di squadra**
* Modalità di lavoro **ibrida,** per promuovere un sano equilibrio tra vita lavorativa e personale
* Programmi di **formazione** e sviluppo avanzati per rimanere sempre aggiornati
* **Mindset internazionale** e **progettualità innovative**
* Iniziative incentrate sul **benessere psicofisico**


Inquadramento ai sensi del CCNL Commercio, RAL indicativa compresa tra € 31\.000 e € 41\.000 per la qualifica di Senior Consultant e tra € 38\.000 e € 51\.000 per la qualifica di Assistant Manager, determinata sulla base di criteri oggettivi con riferimento specifico alle competenze e all’esperienza del profilo rispetto ai requisiti della posizione.



In ottemperanza alla normativa vigente, non raccogliamo né utilizziamo informazioni sulle retribuzioni percepite in rapporti di lavoro attuali o precedenti. Le nostre procedure di selezione sono, inoltre, improntate a criteri oggettivi e neutrali rispetto al genere.

  

***Together for Better – I nostri valori e il nostro impegno***

  



Leadership, lavoro in team, rispetto di ogni individualità, approfondimento continuo, trasparenza, impegno verso la comunità, integrità: questi sono i **Valori di KPMG** che danno vita a un ambiente aperto allo scambio di idee e di conoscenza, che permette ai nostri professionisti di **esprimere tutto il loro potenziale**.



Grazie all’impegno nel favorire un **ambiente di lavoro inclusivo**, il nostro Network risulta conforme agli standard dei requisiti **ISO 30415**, sul Sistema di Gestione per la Diversità e l’Inclusione, e **UNI PdR 125**, relativa alla parità di genere. All’interno del nostro **Impact Plan** \- Our Impact Plan (kpmg.com) \- trovi riassunte tutte le **azioni concrete** che stiamo portando avanti su tematiche ambientali, sociali e di governance (**ESG**).


**Unisciti a noi!**

  


*I tuoi dati saranno trattati nel pieno rispetto delle disposizioni del Regolamento (UE) n. 679/2016 (General Data Protection Regulation \- "GDPR") e successive modifiche. Per ulteriori informazioni, consultare l'informativa sulla privacy di KPMG.*

  


***KPMG Advisory S.p.A. garantisce le pari opportunità.***

  


**Senior Consultant/Assistant Manager \- Governance, Risk \& Compliance \- Financial Services**

  


**Do Work That Matters \- Your role and responsibilities**

  



Do you want to pursue a profession that is truly meaningful and impactful? At KPMG, you will have the opportunity to help clients, the company, and colleagues address and solve the most current and complex challenges. KPMG Advisory offers consulting services to companies to assist them in business model transformation, process improvement and digitisation, risk analysis and management, and support in extraordinary finance transactions.



When you join the Governance, Risk, Compliance and Sustainability Services team, you will help companies transform risks into opportunities for the improvement of their organisation and evolution of their governance systems to gain a competitive edge and offer integrated solutions and innovative approaches for strategic risk management.

  



KPMG Advisory’s Governance, Risk \& Compliance team supports companies in the definition of governance and organisational structures to face the challenges of digital transformation through processes for the management of risks and opportunities. If you are passionate about governance and internal control and risk management systems applied to the financial reporting sector, with an interest in consulting, join our **Milan** team serving clients in the **Financial sector.**

  



Specifically, you will be tasked with:


* Managing the **administrative liability of entities** in accordance with **Legislative Decree 231/01**
* Overseeing and assessing the **internal control systems** of **banking, financial, and insurance intermediaries**

  


***Come As You Are \- Your skills and characteristics***



This is the perfect opportunity for you if:

  


* at least **two years** of **risk \& compliance experience** with companies (insurance companies or banks) or consulting companies;
* Master’s degree or equivalent qualification in **Economics or Law**, with excellent academic results;
* an interest in **financial services** (banking and/or insurance) and a good knowledge of the finance sector's regulatory framework;
* excellent knowledge of **English**, preferably with study and/or work experience abroad.

  


***Your Future With Us \- What you’ll find at KPMG***

  


* An **inclusive** and equitable **work environment** that respects, encourages and values **diversity.**
* A challenging and dynamic environment that encourages **creativity** and **teamwork.**
* **Hybrid** work arrangements to promote a healthy work\-life balance.
* Advanced **training** and development programmes to stay current.
* **International** **mindset** and **innovative projects.**
* Initiatives focused on **mental and physical well\-being.**

  

  

***Together for Better – Our values and commitment***

  



Leadership, teamwork, respect every individual, continuous learning, transparency, commitment to the community and integrity. These are the **KPMG** **values** that create an environment that encourages the sharing of ideas and knowledge, so our professionals can **achieve their full potential**.



As a result of our commitment to fostering an **inclusive work** environment, our Network complies with the standards of the **ISO 30415** requirements, on the Diversity and Inclusion Management System, and **UNI PdR 125**, relating to gender equality. Within our **Impact Plan** \- Our Impact Plan (kpmg.com) \- you will find summarised all the concrete actions we are taking on environmental, social and governance (ESG) issues.


**Join us!**


*Your data will be processed in full compliance with the provisions of Regulation (EU) no. 679/2016 (General Data Protection Regulation \- “GDPR”), as amended. For more information, please see KPMG’s privacy policy.*


***KPMG Advisory S.p.A. is an equal opportunity employer.***

title: Senior Maintenance Manager - Province of Varese
description:
*WSP is the world's leading engineering company, specialising in infrastructure, transport, environment, energy and buildings.*

*We have a clear vision of how it wants to help make the world a better place, by giving back to communities and supporting what's really important for our future.*


Due to significant growth within our Energy business, WSP is seeking to strengthen its **Decommissioning \& Waste Management Programme** team with the appointment of a **Senior** **Maintenance Manager,** based on\-site in the **Province of Varese**.


In this role, you will join a multidisciplinary team that embodies our company's core values: solidarity, a passion for large\-scale and complex projects, and a spirit of innovation and conviviality.

**YOUR NEW ROLE, WHAT'S INVOLVED?**

**YOUR TEAM**


As Maintenance Manager, you will provide technical leadership and operational oversight for maintenance activities delivered by contractors, ensuring alignment with programme objectives, client requirements and regulatory obligations.


Your responsibilities will include:

* Managing, coordinating and supervising preventive, corrective and extraordinary maintenance activities across civil, mechanical, electrical, instrumentation and related disciplines.
* Defining maintenance priorities and intervention plans in collaboration with the Client and Works Management.
* Leading multidisciplinary teams of engineers, technicians and supervisors while providing coaching and mentoring support.
* Reviewing and approving technical maintenance documentation, reports, procedures, work packages and engineering deliverables.
* Supervising dimensional, topographic, photographic and 3D laser scanning surveys and validating outputs for project use.
* Managing technical documentation, records and archiving systems, including Life Records files, in accordance with client quality requirements.
* Monitoring maintenance KPIs, budgets and programme progress, providing regular reporting to internal stakeholders and the Client.
* Acting as the primary technical interface with Client representatives, Works Management and supporting functional units.
* Ensuring compliance with health, safety, radiation protection and quality assurance requirements across all maintenance activities.
* Supporting the wider Decommissioning \& Waste Management Programme and related operational activities as required.

**WHAT IF IT WAS YOU?**


We're looking for a technically strong and collaborative professional who can bring leadership, engineering expertise and a proactive approach to maintenance management. Ideally, you will have:

* A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering or a related technical discipline.
* More than 12 years of professional experience in maintenance management, supervision, and coordination of multidisciplinary engineering activities.
* Experience gained within nuclear, decommissioning, energy, heavy industrial or other highly regulated environments.
* Proven leadership experience managing multidisciplinary teams and contractor organisations.
* Strong knowledge of mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, civil and associated engineering systems.
* Experience reviewing technical documentation, drawings, BIM models and maintenance deliverables.
* Familiarity with maintenance management standards such as EN 13306 and ISO 55000 series.
* Excellent analytical, organisational and problem\-solving skills.
* Fluent English (minimum B1 level) and Italian language capability at B2 level or above.
* Ability to work within classified areas and maintain compliance with all applicable safety and radiation protection requirements.

**WHAT YOU’LL LIKE ABOUT US:**

* A working environment inspired by **diversity**,driven by inclusion.
* WSP is about making the world around us a **better place**, including the environment, transport infrastructure, buildings and land use.
* Excellent **career** prospects. We're growing fast, and every day we strive to outdo ourselves. We are open to your ideas and want to try **new** things. We have a supportive environment that invests in your **development** and training.
* WSP is about embracing a culture of **collaboration** with colleagues who stand out for their **skills**. Come and discover our fantastic team!

**OUR RECRUITMENT PROCESS:**


1\. Telephone interview: We'll start with a telephone interview to get to know you better and discuss your experience.


2\. HR/Manager Interview: You will meet with our TA team and the department manager to assess your technical and cultural skills.


3\. Interview with the Business Unit Director: A meeting with the Business Unit Director to discuss your potential role and our strategic vision.

**ABOUT US:**


We are one of the world's leading engineering and professional services firms. Our trusted employees share a common goal of making a positive and lasting impact on the communities we serve through a culture of innovation, integrity and inclusion.

**Are you hesitating to apply? Go for it!** We are proud to promote diversity, equal opportunities and professional integration within our teams, and we are convinced that our differences and an inclusive working environment contribute to our development. All applications will be carefully considered.

**Compensation \& Benefits**  

WSP is committed to pay transparency. The annual gross salary range for this position is **€55,000 \- €70,000**. The final offer will be determined based on the successful candidate's experience, qualifications and relevant expertise. In addition to base salary, we offer a benefits package and opportunities for professional development.

Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. is the world leader in serving science. Whether our customers are accelerating life sciences research, solving analytical challenges, growing efficiency in their laboratories, improving patient health through diagnostics or the development and manufacture of life\-changing therapies, we are here to support them. Our distributed team delivers an outstanding combination of innovative technologies.


ThermoFisher mRNA unit in Monza is looking for a MSAT Scientist role!

**What will you do?**

* As a member of the Manufacturing Science \& Technology Team, the resource will lead the successful transfer, scale up, supervision and optimization of bioprocesses. You are regarded as a senior expert in process science and manufacturing aspects. In this role, you significantly contribute to the development of the MSAT toolbox and share the manufacturing responsibility as a long\-term process owner.

This includes the whole lifecycle of assigned projects from planning over coordination, implementation, control and project completion aligned with project management goals. Join us, and be part of our Company

title: Quality Engineer
description:
Panoramica:

Magnera's purpose is to better the world with new possibilities made real. For more than 160 years, the originating companies have delivered the material solutions their partners need to thrive. Through economic upheaval, global pandemics and changing end\-user needs, they have consistently found ways to solve problems and exceed expectations. By bringing together these legacy companies, the distinct scale and comprehensive portfolio of products will bring customers more materials and choices. With a combined legacy of resilience, Magnera will build personal partnerships that withstand an ever\-changing world.
Responsabilità:

The Quality Engineer will be responsible for monitoring the progress of product quality controls, analyzing inspection and testing data to identify trends and perform statistical analyses, and communicating results to the production departments. The role also includes managing and sharing quality data through customer portals.
In addition, the Quality Engineer will oversee process control activities, ensuring that standards are in place and properly applied, analyze internal non\-conformities, and support sampling activities as well as the preparation, maintenance, and updating of product Technical Specifications. Participation in audits and close collaboration with the Product Development and Quality Laboratory teams are essential aspects of the position.
The Quality Engineer will also be responsible for managing and sharing analyses related to internal non\-conformities in cooperation with the Quality Analyst and the Quality Manager. The role involves issuing non\-conformity reports within the area of responsibility and delivering training related to quality processes and standards.
Furthermore, the position requires supporting sampling activities, managing the preparation and internal distribution of sampling reports, and coordinating the archiving and management of reference samples. The Quality Engineer will also handle customer requests for finished product samples and provide support in responding to regulatory\-related inquiries from customers.  

a) Monitor the progress of product quality controls to be performed in relation to ongoing production activities and the applicable sampling plan, supporting their execution when necessary.
b) Verify the correct recording of production and laboratory inspection data, determining any additional checks required to ensure product compliance.
c) Monitor and analyze quality data to identify trends and perform statistical analyses on products and their characteristics.
d) Ensure the timely communication of test and inspection results to the production departments.
e) Manage the sharing of analysis data through dedicated customer portals.
f) Review and analyze production and Quality Laboratory test data prior to the issuance of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA).
g) Issue and send Certificates of Analysis (CoA) to customers and ensure their proper filing and archiving.
h) Review and analyze internal non\-conformities raised during production and placed on hold, in collaboration with the Quality Analyst and the Quality Manager, when required.
i) Issue non\-conformity reports within the area of responsibility, including finished product laboratory controls, incoming inspections of externally processed materials and raw materials, and process\-related controls.
j) Support sampling activities related to new products, new raw materials, and other projects, collaborating with the Product Development Department when necessary.
k) Manage the preparation and internal distribution of sampling reports.
l) Coordinate and support the archiving and management of reference samples.
m) Fulfill requests for finished product samples to be sent to customers, when required.
n) Handle customer and internal requests related to regulatory matters.
o) Support the drafting, updating, and internal distribution of Product Technical Specifications.
p) Contribute to the daily communication and reporting of waste and internally recorded non\-conformities, including the management of related files and records.
q) Assist in organizing Quality Laboratory activities and cooperate to ensure adequate laboratory coverage.
r) Collaborate with other Quality Department team members to ensure laboratory equipment remains properly maintained, functional, and calibrated.
s) Support the Quality Manager in the development and updating of procedures, work instructions, and forms directly related to Quality Control activities.
t) Deliver and support training activities within the area of responsibility, ensuring effective employee qualification and competence development.
u) Support the creation of new item codes and related setup activities within the NAVISION ERP system.
v) Assist in the analysis and investigation of customer complaints.
w) Participate in first\-, second\-, and third\-party audits, when required.  


Qualifiche:
**Education*** University degree (Bachelor’s or Master’s degree).

**Language Skills*** Good command of English, both written and spoken.

**Computer Skills*** Good computer literacy, including proficiency in standard business and quality management applications.

**Specific Knowledge*** Knowledge of ISO standards.
* Knowledge of product analysis and measurement methodologies.
* Understanding of statistical techniques and data analysis.
* Knowledge of Root Cause Analysis (RCA) methodologies and problem\-solving tools.

**Other Competencies*** Strong sense of responsibility and accountability.
* Agile mindset and results\-oriented approach.
* Ability to work effectively as part of a team.
* Initiative, proactivity, and a continuous improvement mindset

title: Sustainability specialist internship
description:
At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

**This position reports to:**


Sustainability Manager
\_\_

**Your role and responsibilities**  

  

In this role, you will have the opportunity to contribute to improving sustainability performance in your assigned area of responsibility. Each day, you will support the implementation of the group\-wide sustainability strategy and business\-specific priorities by applying in\-depth knowledge and offering solutions and advice. You will also showcase your expertise by providing feedback to the management and employees on lessons learned and best practices from within your own unit and across ABB.  

  

The work model for the role is: \#LI\-Hybrid  

  

This role is contributing to the smart power division in Bergamo.  

  

You will be mainly accountable for:

* Assisting in reporting sustainability progress with respect to the implementation of the group\-level sustainability objectives and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) as required.
* Supporting and driving behavioral change through the local implementation of group\-wide and business\-specific programs and practices to ensure a strong focus on sustainability matters, across your respective organization.
* Contributing to and executing sustainability\-related projects.
* Developing project objectives and reporting the progress to management.

 *Our team dynamics*  

You will join a high performing international team, where you will be able to thrive.

 **Qualifications for the role**

* You are currently pursuing, or have recently completed, a degree in Engineering or a Scientific discipline.
* You have an interest in sustainability practices, emissions reporting, and regulatory compliance.
* Knowledge of Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) methodology is considered a plus.
* You are passionate about sustainability and demonstrate a proactive approach.
* You possess strong interpersonal and communication skills.
* You are comfortable communicating in both Italian and English.

  

**What's in it for you?**


Benefits – You can count on:

* Mentorship from experienced professionals
* Exposure to modern techniques and best practices
* Access to ABB's comprehensive training resources and certifications

ABB Smart Power provides energy distribution solutions for data centers, industrial and manufacturing plants, critical infrastructure and commercial buildings. The Division’s portfolio includes industrial circuit breakers, low\-voltage systems, motor starting applications, and safety devices like switches and relays. Its Power Protection unit supports the world’s largest data center companies with advanced energy\-efficient UPS solutions. The Division’s ABB Ability™ Energy Manager provides a scalable, easy\-to\-use platform that helps organizations save energy and reduce CO2 emissions.

title: Product Certification Operational Manager – Industrial Safety (Italy)
description:
**Company Description**  

We are SGS – the world's leading testing, inspection and certification company. We are recognized as the global benchmark for sustainability, quality and integrity. Our 99,500 employees operate a network of 2,500 offices and laboratories, working together to enable a better, safer and more interconnected world.

 **Job Description**  

At SGS, we are looking for an experienced Product Certification Operational Manager to lead our Industrial Safety team in Italy.


SGS Industrial Safety operates in the following areas:

* Mechanical Safety (machinery, lifts, pressure vessels)
* Functional and Process Safety
* Structural Safety (construction products)
* Fire \& Explosion Safety (ATEX)
* Electrical Safety
* Non\-Destructive Testing (NDT)


This is a strategic leadership role combining operational management, business development, and technical excellence within the Testing, Inspection \& Certification (TIC) industry. You will be responsible for driving business performance, ensuring compliance with national and international accreditation requirements, and supporting the growth of our Industrial Safety services across Italy and the SGS global network.

**What you'll do**

* Lead the strategic and operational management of Industrial Safety in Italy.
* Take full ownership of operational performance, resource planning, and P\&L.
* Ensure the maintenance and extension of national and international accreditations and regulatory authorizations.
* Develop and expand our Industrial Safety service portfolio in line with market trends and emerging industry needs.
* Lead and develop internal teams and external technical experts, promoting capability development through coaching and collaboration.
* Partner with SGS affiliates worldwide to deliver international projects and strengthen cross\-border collaboration.
* Support commercial growth by contributing to business development and client engagement activities.

  

**Qualifications**  

* Degree in Engineering or another relevant technical discipline.
* Business\-level proficiency in English
* Minimum of 10 years’ experience in TIC, Technical Services, or Operations
* Proven expertise in statutory and regulatory compliance services across competitive markets.
* Practical experience with inspection, testing and conformity assessments under EU regulatory framework, including:
	+ Machinery and Lifts
	+ Pressure Equipment (static and transportable)
	+ ATEX (equipment and hazardous zones)
	+ Construction Products
	+ NDT
* Familiarity with audit practices aligned with EN ISO 19011, ideally verified by a recognized organization.

title: Nestlé Purina PetCare - Operations Start-Up Leader
description:
At Nestlé Purina we are committed to creating richer lives for pets and the people who love them, providing high quality nutrition for cats and dogs and doing more of what matters for pets, people and planet  

We are looking for passionate technical talents who will support the installation, start\-up and operation of modern and highly automated production systems during the launch of a new manufacturing site.  

You will contribute to a project within a global organization committed to quality, safety and continuous improvement.

 **Position Snapshot**

* Nestlé Purina Italy
* Permanent and full\-time role
* Nestlé welcomes people with disabilities
* University Degree in Engineering
* 3\-5 years of experience in successful Production leadership, as Production manager or line supervisor
* Approximately 3\-5 years of experience as factory pillar leader, AM or PM pillar leader
* Fluency in English is required. Italian knowledge would be a plus.
* Compensation Package: CCNL Industria Alimentare, 1° Level, Gross Annual Base Salary €63\.500,00\.

 **Position Summary:**  

As a CAPEX project team member, ensure Vertical start\-up and sustained performance, by proper preparation and leadership of the start\-up phase.

  



**A Day in the life of an** ***Operations Start\-Up Leader*****:**

* In project design phase, assisting in the User Requirement Specifications development by identifying local site operational constraints.
* Coordinate product/process permits \& licenses with the project manager, quality manager and the regulatory team.
* Coordinate/manage the staffing and training process, e.g. conducting the TT\&T program
* Coordinate the Raw/Pack/consumables materials availability and production planning during start\-up phase
* Develop and manage master startup plan for commissioning, qualification and verification phase, designed to achieve a vertical start\-up with sustained performance.
* During project Start\-up phase, coordinate/manage operational team and ensure product, equipment, line and site certifications are completed on time
Ensure a proactive and effective handover of the project to the operational team including the final Manufacturing dossier (MD).  
* 

**What will make you successful?**

* Previous experience in running manufacturing Projects (e.g. Product specialist, Process engineer, Application group…)
* Developed ability to maintain a safe working environment through proper housekeeping (5 S) with safety practices
* Good knowledge of how training and coach people
* Developed ability to ensure working relationships are harmonious and are focused on achieving common objectives.
* Developed ability to elaborate trade\-off for optimal choices.

 **What we offer?**  

At Nestlé we believe that success is driven by people. That’s why we offer a well\-rounded benefits package designed to support well\-being, growth, and quality of life:

* Work\-life balance – flexible working arrangements and hybrid options
* Continuous learning – access to training, certifications, and development programs
* Health \& well\-being – private health insurance and wellness initiatives
* Corporate welfare – flexible benefits platform
* Career growth – internal mobility and development opportunities
* Inclusive culture – collaborative and people\-first environment.

 **What does it mean working at Purina?**  

At Purina, we’ve worked hard to create a high\-performing and caring environment where everyone belongs, and where everyone can push themselves.  

When you join our mission to create richer lives for pets, the people who love them and the planet we share, you’ll find lots of opportunities to develop your skills, knowledge and expertise, both at Purina and across the wider Nestlé community.  

You’ll become part of a passionate and successful international team who, as industry pioneers and leaders in their field, are continually striving for better, who feel safe and supported to challenge, to experiment, to drive change and to be their true selves at work.  

We’re committed to ensuring the whole team can experience the power of Purina, pushing their limits and realising their full potential.  

That’s the Power of Purina.  

Are you ready to discover what’s possible?  

Purina is part of Nestlé Group.

description:

Office**Job Description**


Join Us as a Senior Clinical Team Manager \- Make an Impact at the Forefront of Innovation

  

The Senior Clinical Team Manager is accountable for achieving the final clinical deliverable (usually clean data from evaluable patients as specified in the study protocol) within the time period specified in the contract with the customer. Interprets data on complex issues and makes good business decisions with support from line management. The role involves working to ensure that all clinical deliverables meet the customer's time/quality/cost expectations, and maintaining profitability by ensuring clinical activity is conducted within contract scope, through efficient management of the clinical team. Typically the Senior Clinical Trial Manager works independently on projects of moderate to high complexity, and may assume regional lead or Clinical Study Manager responsibilities.

What You'll Do:

* Manage all clinical operational and quality aspects of allocated studies, of moderate to high complexity, in compliance with ICH GCP. In accordance to project specific requirements, may assume Lead CTM responsibilities on regional level, Clinical Study Manager (CSM) responsibilities or take on additional responsibilities, e.g. process improvements, manage complex programs, have wider management or project coordination responsibilities.

Contribute to the development of the Master Action Plan (MAP) for providing clinical related documents. Ensure timely set up, organization, content and quality of the relevant sections of the Trial Master File (local and central). May participate in the design and development of CRFs, CRF guidelines, patient informed consent templates and other protocol specific documents as required. Continuously monitor clinical trials to assess performance and ensure contractual obligations are met. May routinely conduct Accompanied Field Visits (AFVs) and be accountable for project financials.
